module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 1, Slide 2
question: Which of the following are stated goals of load balancing?
options: Optimize resource utilization
Maximize throughput
Minimize response time
Avoid overload of any single resource
Eliminate the need for network security
correct_answers: Optimize resource utilization
Maximize throughput
Minimize response time
Avoid overload of any single resource

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 3, Slide 5
question: In order to do work, what must a Server Load Balancer (SLB) do?
options: Know servers - IP/port, availability
Understand details of some protocols (e.g., FTP, SIP, etc)
Re-write packets as they pass through via NAT
Assign tasks to different processors using a global pressure grid
correct_answers: Know servers - IP/port, availability
Understand details of some protocols (e.g., FTP, SIP, etc)
Re-write packets as they pass through via NAT

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 4, Slide 7
question: According to the Server Load Balancing Goal, when is a load considered balanced?
options: If no processes are idle
If the throughput reaches 10 Gbps
If all servers have an equal amount of RAM available
If network address translation is disabled
correct_answers: If no processes are idle

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 5, Slide 8
question: The load balancing problem can be reduced to which NP-complete problem?
options: The traveling salesperson problem
The bin-packing problem
The knapsack problem
The longest path problem
correct_answers: The bin-packing problem

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 6, Slide 10
question: Match the load balancing categories to their definitions.
options: Load assigned before application runs
Load assigned as applications run
Application periodically suspended and load balanced

correct_answers.C: Application periodically suspended and load balanced

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 6, Slide 11
question: Match the Server Load Balancing algorithm categories to their descriptions.
options: If they adapt to system load levels using thresholds
If load balancing traffic is independent of load levels
If both senders and receivers initiate action
If load balancing overhead is minimal

correct_answers.A: If they adapt to system load levels using thresholds
correct_answers.B: If load balancing traffic is independent of load levels
correct_answers.C: If both senders and receivers initiate action
correct_answers.D: If load balancing overhead is minimal

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 7, Slide 13
question: Which of the following are listed as disadvantages of Static Load Balancing?
options: Predicting execution times is often not knowable
Affect of communication dynamics is often ignored
The number of iterations required by processors to converge is indeterminate
It requires a global pressure grid
correct_answers: Predicting execution times is often not knowable
Affect of communication dynamics is often ignored
The number of iterations required by processors to converge is indeterminate

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 7, Slide 13
question: Which of the following are examples of Partitioning algorithms used in Static Load Balancing?
options: Recursive Bisection
Simulated Annealing
Genetic Algorithms
Multi-level Contraction and Refinement
Adaptive Contracting
correct_answers: Recursive Bisection
Simulated Annealing
Genetic Algorithms
Multi-level Contraction and Refinement

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 8, Slide 14
question: Match the predominant Server Load-Balancing Algorithms to their operational methods.
options: Server with smallest number of flows gets the new flow request.
Associate a strength for each server and distribute load based on all servers in the farm.
Round robin thru the servers in server farm.
Give each server a specific number of flows in a row.

correct_answers.A: Server with smallest number of flows gets the new flow request.
correct_answers.B: Associate a strength for each server and distribute load based on all servers in the farm.
correct_answers.C: Round robin thru the servers in server farm.
correct_answers.D: Give each server a specific number of flows in a row.

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 8, Slide 15
question: In Centralized Dynamic Load Balancing, what is the term for when processes ask for more work while still working?
options: Double buffering
Adaptive contracting
Receiver initiated balancing
Thrashing
correct_answers: Double buffering

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 9, Slide 16
question: Match the processor types in a Centralized Load Balancing (Work Pool) Algorithm to their tasks.
options: Maintains the work pool (queue, heap, etc.)
Perform task and then ask for another

correct_answers.A: Maintains the work pool (queue, heap, etc.)
correct_answers.B: Perform task and then ask for another

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 9, Slide 17
question: In Decentralized Dynamic Load Balancing, which of the following is true regarding how processes detect their state?
options: Processes detect that their workload is low.
Processes sense an overload condition when new tasks are spawned.
Processes sense an overload condition when a sudden increase in task load occurs.
A master processor monitors all slave states simultaneously.
correct_answers: Processes detect that their workload is low.
Processes sense an overload condition when new tasks are spawned.
Processes sense an overload condition when a sudden increase in task load occurs.

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 10, Slide 19
question: Match the Decentralized Mechanism to when it is most effective.
options: Effective when the average load is heavy
Effective when the average load is light

correct_answers.A: Effective when the average load is heavy
correct_answers.B: Effective when the average load is light

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 10, Slide 19
question: What is a disadvantage of a Sender Initiated decentralized mechanism when loads are heavy?
options: Can cause thrashing
Causes the master node to crash
It requires a request frequency threshold
It ignores the average load completely
correct_answers: Can cause thrashing

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 11, Slide 20
question: Which of the following are true statements regarding Global vs. Local process selection?
options: Global involves all the processors of the network.
Global may require expensive global synchronization.
Local involves only neighbor processes.
Local is easier to manage and has less overhead than the global approach.
Local guarantees that the overall load will always be perfectly balanced.
correct_answers: Global involves all the processors of the network.
Global may require expensive global synchronization.
Local involves only neighbor processes.
Local is easier to manage and has less overhead than the global approach.

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 11, Slide 21
question: In the context of process selection, which neighbor selection algorithm requires issuing bids to neighbors where the best bid wins?
options: Adaptive Contracting
Random
Round Robin
Gradient Algorithm
correct_answers: Adaptive Contracting

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 12, Slide 22
question: When choosing thresholds for a low load condition (when a process is about to go idle), what is the primary goal?
options: Prevent idleness, not achieve perfect balance
Ensure all servers have exactly equal connections
Minimize load balancing overhead at the master node
Issue bids to all network nodes simultaneously
correct_answers: Prevent idleness, not achieve perfect balance

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 12, Slide 23
question: When the system load is high and the goal is to prevent thrashing, what type of threshold works well?
options: An exponentially growing threshold
A low threshold constant
A static global threshold
A randomized threshold limit
correct_answers: An exponentially growing threshold

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 13, Slide 24
question: In the Gradient Algorithm, what Node Data Structures are maintained for each neighbor?
options: Distance, in hops, to the nearest lightly-loaded process
A load status flag indicating if the current processor is lightly-loaded, or normal
The IP address of the master processor
The bid value for the adaptive contracting handshake
correct_answers: Distance, in hops, to the nearest lightly-loaded process
A load status flag indicating if the current processor is lightly-loaded, or normal

module: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks
source: Load Balancing in Cloud and Data Center Networks.pdf, Page 13, Slide 24
question: How is Local Synchronization handled in the Gradient Algorithm?
options: Node status changes are multicast to its neighbors
The master node polls all slaves iteratively
Spawned jobs are sent strictly via random selection
A global pressure grid is rebuilt every millisecond
correct_answers: Node status changes are multicast to its neighbors
